compiled_state.title: Enriched task descriptions: Pattern Reference section
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## User Story
As an implementation-plan user, when a task involves code with low or medium pattern familiarity, I want a `## Pattern Reference` section with codebase examples so sub-agents have concrete implementation patterns.

## Done Definitions
1. Pattern familiarity is read from the story-level complexity evaluator output (Dimension 7) and applies uniformly to all tasks generated from that story. When familiarity is low or medium, implementation-plan retrieves matching code examples from the codebase.
2. Examples capped at 30 lines total, inserted under `## Pattern Reference` section
3. When pattern familiarity is high, section is omitted
4. When no matching examples found, section is omitted
5. Examples dynamically retrieved at task description generation time (not hardcoded)
6. Tests: structural boundary test validates `## Pattern Reference` heading; integration test: given a low-familiarity story, all generated tasks include <=30 lines of code examples in Pattern Reference

## Considerations
- [Performance] Pattern retrieval adds latency to task generation; 30-line cap mitigates but consider timeout/fallback

## Escalation Policy
Escalate when blocked: Agents proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. All assumptions made without escalating are documented.
